{"step_by_step_thinking": "Crigler Najjar syndrome and Gilbert syndrome both involve impaired bilirubin conjugation, leading to increased unconjugated bilirubin. Dubin Johnson syndrome also involves impaired bilirubin conjugation. However, hemolytic anemia primarily results in increased bilirubin production due to the breakdown of red blood cells, rather than impaired conjugation. Therefore, unconjugated bilirubin would be increased in hemolytic anemia.", "answer_choice": "D"}
